phenotypicFeatures.description: Progressive: ~6 adenomas at age 19, accumulating to ~15+ by age 31; stomach, cecum, sigmoid

Stage I papillary thyroid microcarcinoma; clinical: solid 1.1 cm isthmic nodule; pathology: encapsulated, non-invasive, 0.6 cm carcinoma, left middle thyroid lobe; cystic variant with extensive squamous metaplasia; AJCC 8th ed T1aN0M0 (patient <55 years); associated adenomatoid nodular hyperplasia; diagnosed at age ~28; outside established PPAP tumor spectrum

Bilateral proliferative breast changes with PASH; biopsy (2020): columnar cell change, cystic/papillary apocrine metaplasia, stromal fibrosis, focal PASH; IHC: CD34+, CK AE1/AE3- (confirms PASH); left fibroadenomatous change; first diagnosed at age ~27. No specific HPO term for PASH; closest parent term used.

Segment VII, subcapsular, 16x14 mm; peripheral hypervascular in arterial phase, isodense venous phase, no washout; most likely hemangioma vs FNH; associated splenoportal arteriovenous shunt; FNH lacks specific HPO term; incidental finding

Stage IV+ deep infiltrating endometriosis; trans-diaphragmatic penetration; bilateral ovarian endometriomas; ureteral encasement; appendiceal involvement; pelvic free fluid; symptomatic since ~22, Stage IV diagnosis at ~27
Diffuse asymmetric adenomyosis; severe menorrhagia requiring regular iron infusions; caused syncope; necessitated ovarian-sparing total hysterectomy; diagnosed at age ~28
Recurrent SIBO; may result from bowel adhesions and chronic peritoneal inflammation
Gastric dysmotility requiring medication management
Duplicated inferior vena cava; no specific HPO term for duplicated IVC; cannot be explained by somatic mechanisms; argues for constitutive POLE haploinsufficiency
ANA 1:160 with reticular cytoplasmic AC-21 pattern; associated with anti-mitochondrial antibodies
Ultra-hypermutated tumor; TMB >100 mut/Mb; no specific HPO term for tumor mutational burden; broadest applicable parent term used
Chronic gastric gastritis; diffuse gastric inflammation
Bilateral ovarian endometriomas; 5cm right, 2cm left; no specific HPO term for endometrioma; ovarian cyst is closest parent
Retroperitoneal fibrosis with ureteral encasement; required ureterolysis
Hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (hEDS); no specific HPO term for hEDS; HP:0001382 used as closest phenotypic feature
Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS); diagnosed at age ~26
Delayed gastric emptying; overlaps with POLE GI findings
Negative finding; intellectual disability has not been observed
Negative finding; no neurodevelopmental conditions observed
Non-malignant dysplastic nevus; another tissue type showing proliferative dysregulation

persons._comment: No cancer history; brief gout episode as teenager
Not yet tested for POLE c.138del; cascade testing recommended
Maternal grandmother; Stage 1 ductal breast cancer and uterine cancer — uterine cancer is a core PPAP-associated malignancy

disease.label: Colorectal cancer, susceptibility to, 12; CCRC12 (Polymerase proofreading-associated polyposis, PPAP)

extensions.name: variant_effect
extensions.value: frameshift_variant — premature stop at ~residue 54 of 2,286 amino acids, eliminating 100% of functional domains (exonuclease at 268-471, polymerase at 580-1260)

extensions.name: population_frequency
extensions.value: Absent from gnomAD v4 (0/1,614,586 alleles), ClinVar, and all population databases

extensions.name: constraint_metrics
extensions.value: gnomAD pLI = 0.98, LOEUF = 0.22 — extreme loss-of-function intolerance

extensions.name: acmg_evidence_codes
extensions.value: PVS1 (null variant in gene with established LOF mechanism), PM2 (absent from population databases), PP4 (phenotype highly specific for PPAP)

extensions.name: 47_gene_panel_result
extensions.value: Negative for APC, MUTYH, MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2, POLD1, BRCA1/2, TP53, PTEN, and all other genes tested (2022)

term.label: Colorectal cancer, susceptibility to, 12; CCRC12 (Polymerase proofreading-associated polyposis, PPAP)

externalReferences.description: Mur et al. (2023) Genome Medicine 15:85 — Gene-specific ACMG/AMP framework for POLE/POLD1 exonuclease domain variants
Robinson et al. (2021) Nature Genetics — NanoSeq demonstrates germline POLE mutations elevate SBS10a/b in normal cells
